personas: Persona 1: Primary user with basic technical skills seeking efficient access to core features.
Persona 2: Advanced user requiring detailed data and customization options.
Persona 3: Accessibility-focused user needing screen reader support and keyboard navigation.
key_flows: Flow 1: User logs in, navigates to dashboard, completes primary task, and logs out.
Flow 2: User searches for specific content, filters results, and saves favorites.
Flow 3: User accesses settings to customize preferences and notification options.
friction_points: Point 1: Confusing navigation labels leading to difficulty finding key features.
Point 2: Lack of feedback during long loading times causing uncertainty.
Point 3: Inconsistent component behaviors across devices impacting usability.

information_architecture.hierarchy: Content is structured with a clear top-level hierarchy emphasizing primary user tasks, followed by secondary information and settings. Dashboard is the central hub, supported by search, content categories, and user profile management.
navigation: Primary navigation: Dashboard, Search, Categories, Favorites, Settings.
Secondary navigation: User profile menu with account and help options.
categorization: Category 1: Core features and tools accessible from the dashboard.
Category 2: Content organized by type and user relevance within categories.

components: Left column: vertical navigation menu and filters
Right column: main content area with dashboard widgets or content listings
Top bar: global search, notifications, and user profile access

responsive.mobile: Single-column layout with collapsible hamburger menu for navigation, content stacked vertically, and fixed top bar with search and profile icons.
responsive.tablet: Two-column layout with collapsible sidebar, content area adapts width, and top bar remains fixed.
responsive.desktop: Full two-column layout with persistent sidebar navigation and full content width in main area.

design_system.typography: Primary font: Sans-serif, 16px base size with scaling for headings (H1: 32px, H2: 24px, H3: 20px), line height 1.5, and letter spacing 0.02em.
design_system.colors: Palette includes primary blue (#005FCC), secondary gray (#6E6E6E), background white (#FFFFFF), error red (#D93025), success green (#188038), and accessible accent colors for interactive elements.
design_system.spacing: Base spacing unit of 8px, with multiples used for margins and paddings (e.g., 8px, 16px, 24px, 32px). Consistent gutter spacing between columns and components.
components: Button: Primary, secondary, disabled states with clear sizing and padding.
Input fields: standard, focus, error states with labels and helper text.
Cards: content containers with shadows and rounded corners.
Navigation menu: vertical with active item highlight and hover states.

accessibility.keyboard_nav: All interactive components reachable and operable via keyboard; logical tab order; visible focus indicators on all focusable elements.
accessibility.screen_reader: Semantic HTML elements used; ARIA roles and labels applied where necessary; dynamic content updates announced appropriately.
accessibility.contrast_ratio: Minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for text and interactive elements against background.

interactions.animations: Subtle transitions on hover and focus states for buttons and navigation (duration 150ms, ease-in-out). Smooth content fade-in on page load and modal open/close.
interactions.loading_state: Centered spinner animation with descriptive text indicating loading progress; skeleton placeholders for content areas.
interactions.empty_state: Informative message with icon and call-to-action button guiding users to create or find content.
